reference: McKesson Pharmacy Systems — MedSync Program; NCPA Medication Synchronization Guidelines

description: Date on which the patient was enrolled in the MedSync program. Used for program tenure tracking and cohort analysis. Nullable when medsync_enrolled is False.

The designated monthly calendar date on which all synchronized prescriptions for this patient are scheduled for pickup or delivery. Nullable when medsync_enrolled is False. Used for pharmacy workload planning and inventory pre-positioning.

Indicates whether the patient is eligible for the 340B Drug Pricing Program, which provides discounted outpatient drugs to qualifying patients of covered entities. Non-PHI eligibility flag used for claims routing and pricing.
reference: HRSA (Health Resources and Services Administration) — 340B Drug Pricing Program; 42 U.S.C. § 256b

description: Current lifecycle status of the pharmacy patient profile. 'active' indicates the patient is actively using Grocery pharmacy services. 'inactive' indicates no recent activity. 'on_hold' indicates a temporary suspension of services. 'transferred' indicates the patient has moved to another pharmacy. 'deceased' is set upon notification of patient death for record retention compliance.
reference: State Boards of Pharmacy — Patient Record Retention Requirements

description: Classification of the pharmacy patient by service type. 'retail' is the standard in-store patient. 'mail_order' indicates the patient primarily uses mail-order fulfillment. 'specialty' indicates enrollment in specialty pharmacy programs for high-cost or complex medications. 'long_term_care' indicates a patient in a care facility. 'pediatric' indicates a minor patient with a guardian on file.
reference: McKesson Pharmacy Systems — Patient Classification; NCPDP — Patient Type Codes

description: Patient's preferred communication channel for prescription-related notifications, including ready-for-pickup alerts, refill reminders, and drug interaction notices. Drives outbound messaging routing in McKesson and NCR POS Systems.
reference: McKesson Pharmacy Systems — Patient Communication Preferences

description: Patient's preferred method for collecting ready prescriptions. Supports omnichannel fulfillment including in-store counter, curbside pickup (Click-and-Collect), drive-through pharmacy window, and home delivery. Drives OMS (Order Management System) routing for pharmacy fulfillment.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Order Management; McKesson Pharmacy Systems — Fulfillment Preferences

business_glossary_term: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) Benefit Eligibility Flag
description: Indicates whether the patient has been identified as eligible for SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) benefits, which may affect OTC product eligibility at the pharmacy counter. Non-PHI flag aligned with the shopper master SNAP eligibility field.
reference: USDA — SNAP Program Regulations (7 CFR Part 278)

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the pharmacy patient profile record in the Grocery lakehouse Silver layer. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) — Internal Controls; HIPAA Privacy Rule — Record Retention

business_glossary_term: Women, Infants, and Children (WIC) Benefit Eligibility Flag
description: Indicates whether the patient has been identified as eligible for WIC (Women, Infants, and Children) program benefits, which may affect eligible OTC and formula purchases at the pharmacy counter. Non-PHI flag.
reference: USDA — WIC Program Regulations (7 CFR Part 246)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each shopper preference record in the Grocery customer preference data product. Serves as the primary key for all preference-related joins and lineage tracking.

Foreign key linking to assortment.category. Business justification: Category-level shopper preference drives personalization engines, targeted promotions, and assortment decisions in retail-grocery (e.g., organic Produce preference, Dairy avoidance). product_category_
Foreign key linking to store.department. Business justification: PROMOTION: Department‑level preference allows personalized promotions and shelf‑stock decisions per department.

Foreign key linking to customer.household. Business justification: Household-level preferences are a distinct and important concept in grocery retail — dietary restrictions for the whole household, preferred fulfillment channel for household deliveries, private label

Foreign key linking to product.product_item. Business justification: Personalized marketing uses shopper product preferences; linking preferences to product items enables recommendation engines and targeted promotions.

Reference to the loyalty program enrollment record associated with this preference, when the preference was inferred from or is linked to loyalty program behavior. Enables correlation between loyalty tier, points activity, and preference evolution for CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) analytics.
reference: NCR POS Systems — Loyalty Integration; Internal Loyalty Program Management

description: Reference to the shopper or household account that owns this preference record. Links to the customer master in the Customer domain.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Customer Profile Object

description: Reference to the shopper's preferred physical store location for in-store shopping, pickup, or click-and-collect fulfillment. Used by the personalization engine to surface store-specific promotions, ad circulars, and inventory availability. Links to the Store domain.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Store Locator; NCR POS Systems — Loyalty Integration

description: Specifies a product certification standard that the shopper prefers when purchasing. Used to filter product catalog results in digital commerce and to drive assortment recommendations. Aligns with USDA organic certification and GS1 product attribute standards. Null for non-certification preference categories.
reference: USDA National Organic Program (NOP); GS1 Global Product Classification

description: A numeric score between 0.0000 and 1.0000 representing the system's confidence in the accuracy of an inferred preference. A score of 1.0000 indicates a self-declared preference with full certainty. Lower scores indicate inferred preferences with varying degrees of certainty. Used by the personalization engine to weight recommendations.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Personalization Framework; Internal AI/ML Model Governance

description: Indicates whether the shopper has provided explicit consent for Grocery to collect, store, and use their preference data for personalization and targeted marketing purposes. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance. When FALSE, preference data must not be used for marketing purposes.
reference: GDPR Article 7 (Conditions for Consent); CCPA Section 1798.100; FTC Act Section 5

description: The date and time when the shopper provided or last renewed consent for preference data collection and use. Stored in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Required for GDPR and CCPA audit trails.
reference: GDPR Article 7 (Conditions for Consent); CCPA Section 1798.100

description: The version identifier of the privacy policy or consent notice under which the shopper's preference consent was collected (e.g., 'v2.1'). Enables compliance teams to identify shoppers who consented under older policy versions and trigger re-consent workflows when policies are updated.
reference: GDPR Article 7; CCPA Section 1798.100; FTC Privacy Policy Standards

description: The date and time when this preference record was first created in the system. Stored in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and GDPR data subject access request (DSAR) responses.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Storage Limitation); Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Classifies the nature of a dietary restriction preference. 'allergy' indicates a medically significant food allergy. 'intolerance' indicates a food sensitivity. 'medical' indicates a clinically prescribed dietary requirement. 'religious' indicates a faith-based dietary rule (e.g., kosher, halal). 'ethical' indicates a values-based choice (e.g., vegan). 'lifestyle' indicates a personal wellness choice (e.g., keto). Null for non-dietary preference categories.
reference: FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA); USDA Organic Certification Standards

business_glossary_term: Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT) Eligible Filter Flag
description: Indicates whether the shopper has enabled the EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer) eligible product filter in their digital commerce experience. When TRUE, product search and browse results are filtered to show only EBT-eligible items. Supports SNAP/WIC program compliance in the digital channel.
reference: USDA Food and Nutrition Service — SNAP Online Purchasing Pilot; Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Product Filtering

description: The shopper's declared preference for how often they wish to receive promotional email communications, including ad circulars and personalized offers. Governs email suppression logic in the CRM platform. Null for non-communication preference categories.
reference: CAN-SPAM Act; GDPR Article 7 (Consent); Salesforce Commerce Cloud — CRM Integration

description: The version identifier of the machine learning model used to infer this preference from purchase history or behavioral data (e.g., 'v1.3.2'). Null for self-declared preferences. Enables model governance, A/B testing of inference models, and rollback of preferences generated by deprecated model versions.
reference: Internal AI/ML Model Governance; Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Personalization Engine

description: Indicates whether this preference record is currently active and should be applied in personalization, product filtering, and marketing targeting. Set to FALSE when a shopper removes or overrides a preference.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Customer Profile Management

description: Indicates whether this preference represents a food allergy or intolerance (e.g., nut allergy, lactose intolerance, celiac/gluten-free). When TRUE, this preference is treated with elevated priority in product filtering and warning displays to protect shopper safety. Aligns with FDA food allergen labeling requirements.
reference: FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA); FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA)

description: The date and time when this preference record was most recently modified, either by the shopper or by the inference engine. Stored in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used to identify stale inferred preferences that may need re-evaluation.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Customer Profile Management; GDPR Article 5 (Storage Limitation)

description: Identifies the shopper's declared or inferred lifestyle diet pattern. Used to curate personalized product recommendations, recipe suggestions, and category affinities in the digital commerce experience. Null for non-lifestyle preference categories.
reference: USDA Dietary Guidelines for Americans; Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Personalization Framework

description: Free-text field for additional context or clarification about this preference record, entered by a customer service associate or captured from a shopper survey. Examples include specific allergen details beyond the standard type codes, or nuanced dietary requirements not covered by enumerated fields.
reference: Internal Customer Service Standards; Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Customer Profile Management

description: Specifies the primary nutritional goal the shopper is pursuing. Used to filter product catalog results and surface nutritionally aligned products in digital commerce. Aligns with FDA nutrition labeling standards. Null for non-nutritional preference categories.
reference: FDA Nutrition Labeling and Education Act (NLEA); FDA Food Labeling Regulations (21 CFR Part 101)

description: Free-text or coded reason explaining why a preference was overridden. Populated when preference_status is 'overridden', typically when a self-declared preference supersedes an inferred one, or when a customer service associate corrects a preference on behalf of the shopper. Supports audit and data quality review.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; GDPR Article 22 (Automated Decision-Making)

description: Indicates whether this preference record is currently eligible to be consumed by the Salesforce Commerce Cloud personalization engine for product recommendations, digital commerce filtering, and targeted marketing. May be FALSE if consent is withdrawn, the preference is expired, or the confidence score is below the minimum threshold.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Personalization Framework; GDPR Article 22 (Automated Decision-Making)

description: High-level grouping that classifies the type of preference being recorded. Drives routing to the correct personalization engine module in Salesforce Commerce Cloud. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dietary|lifestyle|nutritional_goal|certification|shopping|communication|product_affinity — promote to reference product if additional categories are added]

Indicates how this preference was captured. 'self_declared' means the shopper explicitly set it via the app or web profile. 'purchase_inferred' means it was derived from POS transaction history. 'loyalty_inferred' means it was derived from loyalty program behavior. 'app_behavior' means it was inferred from digital browsing or filtering actions in Salesforce Commerce Cloud.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Personalization Framework; GDPR Article 5 (Data Minimisation)

description: Current lifecycle status of the preference record. 'active' means the preference is in use by the personalization engine. 'inactive' means the shopper has disabled it. 'pending_review' means the inferred preference awaits confidence threshold validation. 'overridden' means a self-declared preference has superseded an inferred one. 'expired' means the preference has passed its validity window.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Customer Profile Management; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Specific preference dimension within the category (e.g., 'nut_allergy', 'gluten_free', 'vegan', 'keto', 'low_sodium', 'organic', 'preferred_store', 'email_frequency', 'produce_affinity'). Granular discriminator used by the personalization engine for product filtering and targeted marketing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: nut_allergy|lactose_intolerance|gluten_free|vegan|vegetarian|keto|paleo|mediterranean|low_sodium|low_sugar|high_protein|organic|non_gmo|usda_certified|preferred_store|preferred_fulfillment|preferred_day|preferred_time|email_frequency|push_notification|category_affinity — promote to reference product]
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Preference Taxonomy; FDA Food Labeling Regulations

description: The declared or inferred value for this preference dimension. For boolean preferences (e.g., nut allergy) this is 'true' or 'false'; for enumerated preferences (e.g., preferred fulfillment) this is the selected option (e.g., 'pickup', 'delivery'); for affinity scores this is a numeric string. Flexible string to accommodate all preference types.

The shopper's preferred method for receiving their grocery orders. 'pickup' refers to curbside or in-store order collection. 'click_and_collect' refers to online order with in-store pickup. Feeds the Salesforce Commerce Cloud Order Management System (OMS) default fulfillment selection and Last Mile Delivery (LMD) routing. Null for non-shopping preference categories.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Order Management System (OMS); Internal Omnichannel Fulfillment Standards

description: The day of the week the shopper most prefers to shop, either declared via app profile or inferred from POS transaction history. Used to time promotional communications (ad circulars, push notifications) and to optimize pickup/delivery slot recommendations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: monday|tuesday|wednesday|thursday|friday|saturday|sunday — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Personalization Framework; NCR POS Systems — Transaction History

description: The time-of-day window the shopper most prefers to shop, either declared or inferred from purchase history. Used to schedule push notifications and delivery/pickup slot suggestions. Complements preferred_shopping_day for precise timing of personalized outreach.

Indicates whether the shopper has a declared or inferred preference for Grocery's private-label (store-owned brand) products over national CPG (Consumer Packaged Goods) brands. Used to surface private-label alternatives in product recommendations and to support private-label brand development analytics.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Private Label Brand Management; Internal Private Label Strategy

description: Indicates whether the shopper has opted in to receive push notifications via the Grocery mobile app. When TRUE, the shopper is eligible to receive personalized push alerts for promotions, order status updates, and loyalty rewards. Governed by mobile consent regulations.
reference: GDPR Article 7 (Consent); CAN-SPAM Act; Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Mobile Push Notification Module

description: Comma-separated list of push notification topic categories the shopper has opted into (e.g., 'promotions,order_updates,loyalty_rewards,weekly_ad'). Allows granular control over notification content beyond the binary opt-in flag. Managed via the Salesforce Commerce Cloud personalization module.
reference: GDPR Article 7 (Consent); Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Personalization Framework

business_glossary_term: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) / Women Infants and Children (WIC) Participant Flag
description: Indicates whether the shopper participates in SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) or WIC (Women Infants and Children) federal benefit programs. When TRUE, the personalization engine surfaces SNAP/WIC-eligible products and EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer) payment options. Treated as confidential due to program participation sensitivity.
reference: USDA Food and Nutrition Service — SNAP Program Regulations (7 CFR Part 271); USDA WIC Program Regulations (7 CFR Part 246)

description: The digital or physical channel through which this preference was captured or inferred. Distinguishes between mobile app self-declaration, web profile updates, in-store kiosk entry, POS transaction inference, and loyalty portal updates. Used for channel attribution in preference data quality reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mobile_app|web|in_store_kiosk|associate_entry|pos|loyalty_portal|survey — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Omnichannel Framework; NCR POS Systems — Loyalty Integration

description: The date after which this preference record is no longer valid and should be excluded from personalization logic. Null indicates an open-ended preference with no expiry. Supports temporary dietary restrictions, seasonal preferences, and time-limited program participation.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Personalization Framework

description: The date from which this preference record is considered valid and should be applied in personalization and product filtering. Supports time-bounded preferences such as seasonal dietary goals or temporary dietary restrictions.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Personalization Framework

description: Unique surrogate identifier for a customer segment record in the Grocery CRM and marketing analytics platform. Primary key for the customer_segment reference entity.

Foreign key linking to store.banner. Business justification: In retail-grocery, customer segments are scoped per banner (e.g., Kroger vs. Ralphs vs. Fred Meyer) to drive banner-specific loyalty programs, circular ad campaigns, and CRM differentiation. A domain

Foreign key linking to store.format. Business justification: Format-based customer segmentation drives circular ad targeting, planogram-aligned promotions, and format-specific CRM campaigns. Retail-grocery domain experts define segments by store format (e.g., s

Indicates whether shoppers in this segment are known to respond to weekly promotional ad circulars (print or digital). Used to prioritize segment inclusion in weekly ad circular targeting and Temporary Price Reduction (TPR) campaigns.
reference: Internal Promotions Management Policy; FTC Advertising Practices

description: The shopping channel(s) to which this segment applies for targeting and personalization. 'digital' applies to Salesforce Commerce Cloud eCommerce and app experiences; 'in_store' applies to NCR POS loyalty and in-store promotions; 'all' applies across omnichannel touchpoints.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Omnichannel Framework; NCR POS Loyalty Integration

description: Name or username of the individual who formally approved this segment for activation. Supports governance audit trails and accountability for marketing targeting decisions.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; FTC Advertising Practices

description: The date on which this segment definition was formally approved by the owning team or governance committee for use in live marketing and personalization activities. Null if the segment has not yet been approved.
reference: Internal CRM Governance Policy; FTC Advertising Practices

description: Categorical band representing the typical transaction basket size (number of items or spend level) for shoppers in this segment. Used for assortment planning, endcap and gondola placement decisions, and targeted upsell promotions.
reference: NCR POS Systems; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Numeric priority ranking used to resolve conflicts when a customer qualifies for multiple segments simultaneously. Lower values indicate higher priority. Used by the CRM and Salesforce Commerce Cloud personalization engine to determine which segment's offer or experience takes precedence.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Personalization Engine; Internal CRM Governance Policy

description: The Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) tier associated with this segment, used to prioritize marketing investment and personalization depth. Platinum represents the highest-value shoppers; unclassified indicates segments not defined by CLTV criteria.
reference: Internal CRM Analytics Framework; Salesforce Commerce Cloud

description: The date and time when this segment record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and governance tracking.
reference: SOX Section 404; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Categorical band representing the digital engagement level of shoppers in this segment, derived from app usage, click-and-collect frequency, and eCommerce activity on Salesforce Commerce Cloud. Used to tailor digital vs. in-store communication strategies.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Analytics; Internal Digital Commerce KPI Framework

description: The calendar date on which this segment expires or is scheduled to be deactivated. Null indicates an open-ended segment with no planned expiration. Used to manage seasonal or campaign-specific segments.

The calendar date on which this segment became or is scheduled to become active for use in campaigns, personalization, and targeting. Supports time-bounded segment activation aligned with promotional calendars.

The identifier for this segment as known in the originating external system (e.g., Salesforce CRM segment ID, NCR loyalty group code). Used for cross-system reconciliation and integration mapping during ETL and API synchronization.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud; NCR POS Systems; Internal Integration Standard

description: Indicates whether members of this segment are eligible for Fuel Rewards program benefits, including per-gallon discounts at Grocery fuel centers. Used to target fuel-related promotions and cross-sell fuel center offers.
reference: Internal Fuel Center Operations Policy; NCR POS Loyalty Integration

description: The geographic scope at which this segment is defined and applied. National segments apply across all Grocery markets; regional segments apply to specific trade areas; local and store_cluster segments apply to specific store groups for hyper-local targeting.
reference: Internal Store Operations Standard; Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Indicates whether this segment targets pharmacy patients or customers with pharmacy-related behaviors (e.g., prescription refill frequency, OTC purchase patterns). Pharmacy segments must be handled in compliance with HIPAA privacy requirements for non-PHI customer data.
reference: HIPAA Privacy Rule 45 CFR Part 164; McKesson Pharmacy Systems

description: Indicates whether this segment is associated with a government-regulated assistance program such as SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program), WIC (Women, Infants, and Children), or EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer). Regulatory program segments require special handling for compliance and reporting.
reference: USDA SNAP Program Regulations; USDA WIC Program Regulations; FTC

description: Indicates whether this segment is used as a suppression list to exclude customers from specific campaigns or communications (e.g., opt-out customers, recently converted customers, do-not-contact lists). Suppression segments are applied as exclusion filters in campaign execution.
reference: FTC CAN-SPAM Act; GDPR Article 21; Internal Marketing Compliance Policy

description: The date and time when this segment record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking, cache invalidation in personalization engines, and audit compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Indicates whether this segment is linked to the Grocery loyalty program for targeted rewards, personalized offers, and NCR POS loyalty integration. Loyalty-linked segments receive differentiated promotional treatment at checkout.
reference: NCR POS Loyalty Integration; Internal Loyalty Program Policy

description: The configured upper limit on the number of customers that can be assigned to this segment at any given time. Used for capacity-constrained segments such as exclusive loyalty tiers or pilot program cohorts. Null indicates no cap.
reference: Internal CRM Governance Policy; Salesforce Commerce Cloud

description: Free-text field for additional context, caveats, or operational notes about this segment provided by the owning team. May include information about known data quality issues, special handling instructions, or historical context not captured in structured fields.

Net Promoter Score (NPS) classification band associated with this segment, indicating the general advocacy level of shoppers in the segment. Promoters are highly satisfied advocates; detractors are at-risk shoppers. Used to prioritize retention and win-back campaigns.
reference: Internal NPS Measurement Framework; CRM Analytics

description: Indicates whether shoppers in this segment show a preference for USDA-certified organic products. Used to drive organic assortment decisions, planogram placement, and targeted promotions for organic categories.
reference: USDA National Organic Program (NOP); Oracle Retail Merchandising System

description: Email address of the primary business owner or data steward responsible for this segment. Used for governance notifications, approval workflows, and segment lifecycle management communications.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; GDPR Article 4

description: Name of the internal business team responsible for defining, maintaining, and governing this segment (e.g., 'CRM Analytics', 'Marketing Strategy', 'Loyalty Programs', 'Pharmacy Marketing'). Used for accountability and data stewardship.

The order fulfillment method most commonly used by shoppers in this segment. Pickup = curbside or click-and-collect; delivery = last-mile delivery (LMD); in_store = traditional in-store shopping. Used to optimize omnichannel campaign targeting and OMS routing.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud OMS; Internal Omnichannel Fulfillment Policy

description: Indicates whether shoppers in this segment demonstrate a preference for Grocery's private-label (store-owned brand) products. Used to target private-label promotions, ad circular placements, and category management decisions.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; Internal Private Label Brand Strategy

description: Categorical band representing the typical purchase frequency of shoppers in this segment. Used for lifecycle marketing, replenishment-driven promotions, and churn risk identification. Lapsed indicates shoppers who have not purchased within a defined recency window.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning; Internal CRM Analytics Framework

description: How frequently the membership of this segment is recalculated and refreshed based on updated customer behavior and transaction data. Real-time segments are updated via streaming; manual segments require explicit re-run by the owning team.
reference: Internal CRM Analytics Framework; Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Identifies the specific government assistance program associated with this segment when is_regulatory_program is true. SNAP = Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program; WIC = Women, Infants, and Children; EBT = Electronic Benefits Transfer. NONE if not a regulatory program segment.
reference: USDA SNAP Program Regulations; USDA WIC Program Regulations

description: Short, externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the segment across systems (e.g., HCL-001, SNAP-EBT, ORG-ENT). Used as the business key in CRM integrations, Salesforce Commerce Cloud personalization rules, and NCR POS loyalty targeting.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Segment Management; Internal CRM Naming Standard

description: Detailed narrative description of the segment's defining criteria, business purpose, and intended use cases. Authored by the owning marketing or CRM analytics team for documentation and governance purposes.

Human-readable name of the market segment as displayed in CRM dashboards, marketing campaign tools, and personalization engines (e.g., 'High-CLTV Loyalists', 'SNAP/EBT Shoppers', 'Organic Enthusiasts', 'Fuel Rewards Members').
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud; Internal CRM Standard

description: Current lifecycle status of the segment. Active segments are used in live campaigns and personalization. Draft segments are being defined. Under_review segments are pending approval. Archived segments are retained for historical analysis but no longer applied.

Classification of the segmentation methodology used to define this segment. Behavioral segments are based on purchase patterns; demographic on age/income/household; value-based on Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) tiers; lifecycle on shopper journey stage; dietary_preference on food choices (organic, gluten-free, etc.).
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Segmentation Framework; CRM Analytics Best Practice

description: The operational system of record from which this segment definition originated or is primarily managed. Supports data lineage tracking and system-of-record governance across Grocery's technology landscape.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; Salesforce Commerce Cloud; NCR POS Systems

description: Monotonically incrementing version number for this segment definition. Incremented each time the segment criteria, name, or configuration is materially changed. Supports segment versioning for A/B testing, campaign attribution, and audit history.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; SOX Section 404

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each shopper-to-segment assignment record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for this transactional association entity.

Reference to the household grouping to which the shopper belongs at the time of segment assignment. Enables household-level segmentation analysis and supports promotions targeting at the household unit rather than the individual shopper level.
reference: Internal Customer Household Linkage Standard; Salesforce Commerce Cloud – Household Object

description: Foreign key linking to loyalty.membership. Business justification: Loyalty-tier-driven segmentation requires tracking which specific membership triggered a segment assignment. Loyalty marketing reports (e.g., Gold-tier segment performance) depend on this link. segmen

Foreign key linking to pricing.price_rule. Business justification: Segment-based personalized pricing: when a shopper is assigned to a segment, the governing price rule determines their eligible pricing (loyalty member price, SNAP pricing, digital-only price). Grocer
Reference to the customer segment definition to which the shopper is being assigned. Links to the segment master in the Customer domain.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud – Segment Object; Internal CRM Standard

description: Reference to the loyalty program under which this segment assignment was created. Segments may be scoped to specific loyalty programs (e.g., standard rewards, premium tier, fuel rewards). Null if the assignment is not tied to a specific loyalty program.
reference: Internal Loyalty and Promotions Management Standard; NCR POS – Loyalty Integration

description: Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Campaign-triggered segmentation is a named CRM process in grocery retail: a promo campaign execution triggers re-segmentation of shoppers (e.g., lapsed-buyer win-back campaign moves shoppers into a ne
Reference to the grocery shopper (customer) who is being assigned to the segment. Links to the customer master profile in the Customer domain.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud – Customer Object; Internal CRM Standard

description: Foreign key linking to assortment.store_cluster. Business justification: Cluster-specific segment execution: retail-grocery personalization and targeted promotion systems apply segment assignments at the store cluster level to match cluster assortment. Drives cluster-scope

Reference to the primary store associated with this segment assignment. Captures the shopper's home store or the store context in which the assignment was triggered. Supports store-level segmentation reporting and localized promotion targeting.
reference: Internal Store Operations Standard; NCR POS – Store Configuration

description: Foreign key linking to customer.wholesale_account. Business justification: Wholesale accounts are segmentable business entities — a grocery retailer segments its B2B accounts by account tier, purchase frequency, product category affinity, and geographic market for targeted w

Designates the experimental group to which this shopper-segment assignment belongs when the assignment is part of an A/B or multivariate segmentation test. 'control' is the baseline group; 'treatment_a' and 'treatment_b' are variant groups; 'holdout' is excluded from all treatments for measurement purposes. Null if the assignment is not part of an active test.
reference: Internal Customer Analytics and Personalization Standard

description: The calendar date on which the shopper was formally assigned to the segment. Used for time-series segment membership tracking and cohort analysis.

The mechanism by which the shopper was assigned to this segment. 'rule_based' indicates a deterministic business rule engine; 'ml_model' indicates a machine learning model prediction; 'manual_override' indicates a CRM analyst or marketer directly assigned the shopper; 'batch_import' indicates a bulk load from an external system; 'api_push' indicates a real-time API-driven assignment from an integrated platform such as Salesforce Commerce Cloud.
reference: Internal Customer Analytics and Personalization Standard; Salesforce Commerce Cloud – Segmentation API

description: The operational system of record that originated this segment assignment. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across integrated platforms. Values correspond to known operational systems: Salesforce Commerce Cloud CRM, NCR POS loyalty integration, Blue Yonder demand planning, internal ML platform, manual CRM tooling, or batch ETL processes.
reference: Internal Data Lineage Policy; Internal Systems of Record Catalogue

description: Current lifecycle state of this shopper-to-segment assignment. 'active' means the shopper currently belongs to the segment; 'expired' means the assignment passed its expiry date; 'revoked' means it was manually removed; 'pending' means awaiting activation; 'superseded' means replaced by a newer assignment for the same segment.

The precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the assignment event was recorded in the system. Supports real-time segmentation pipelines and audit trails. Distinct from assignment_date which captures the business date.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; SOX Section 404 – Audit Trail Requirements

description: The identifier of the batch processing run (e.g., Blue Yonder or internal ML platform job execution) that generated this segment assignment. Enables traceability of bulk assignment events back to a specific pipeline execution for debugging, reprocessing, and data lineage auditing. Null for real-time API-driven or manual assignments.
reference: Internal Data Lineage Policy; Blue Yonder Demand Planning – Batch Job Management

description: Defines the omnichannel shopping context in which this segment assignment is applicable. 'all_channels' applies across all touchpoints; 'in_store' applies only to physical store interactions; 'digital' applies to eCommerce and app; 'pickup' applies to Click-and-Collect (curbside or in-store order collection); 'delivery' applies to last-mile delivery orders; 'pharmacy' applies to pharmacy-specific interactions. Supports Grocery's omnichannel personalization strategy.
reference: Internal Omnichannel Strategy Standard; Salesforce Commerce Cloud – Channel Configuration

description: The Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) tier associated with this segment assignment at the time of assignment. Captures the shopper's estimated long-term revenue value tier as determined by the segmentation model or rule engine. Used to prioritize high-value shopper targeting in promotions and personalization. Values: platinum (highest), gold, silver, bronze, unclassified.
reference: Internal Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) Framework; Salesforce Commerce Cloud – Einstein Analytics

description: A numeric probability score between 0.0000 and 1.0000 representing the model's or rule engine's confidence that the shopper belongs to this segment. Populated for ml_model and rule_based assignment methods. Used to filter high-confidence assignments for precision targeting and to support A/B testing of segmentation strategies.
reference: Internal ML Model Governance Standard; Salesforce Commerce Cloud – Einstein Segmentation

description: The date and time when this segment assignment record was first created in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the audit trail creation marker. Distinct from assignment_timestamp which captures the business event time.
reference: SOX Section 404 – Audit Trail Requirements; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Indicates whether the shopper has provided consent for their data to be used in segmentation and targeted marketing activities at the time of this assignment. True means consent is on record. Supports CCPA and GDPR-aligned data privacy compliance for customer data usage in the Customer domain.
reference: CCPA – California Consumer Privacy Act; GDPR Article 6 – Lawfulness of Processing; FTC – Privacy and Data Security

description: The date from which the shopper's membership in this segment is considered active and eligible for targeted promotions, personalization, and loyalty offers. May differ from assignment_date if the assignment is scheduled for a future activation.
reference: Internal Customer Segmentation Governance Policy; FTC – Advertising and Pricing Practices

description: The date on which the shopper's membership in this segment expires and is no longer valid for targeting. Null indicates an open-ended assignment with no scheduled expiry. Supports time-bounded segmentation strategies such as seasonal or promotional cohorts.
reference: Internal Customer Segmentation Governance Policy; FTC – Advertising and Pricing Practices

description: Indicates whether this segment assignment represents the shopper's primary or dominant segment when a shopper belongs to multiple segments simultaneously. True means this is the primary segment used for default personalization and offer targeting. Supports single-segment fallback logic in downstream marketing and POS systems.
reference: Internal Customer Segmentation Governance Policy; Salesforce Commerce Cloud – Personalization Rules

description: The version identifier of the ML model or rule engine that produced this segment assignment. Enables reproducibility, model performance tracking, and rollback analysis. Format follows semantic versioning (e.g., 'v2.3.1') or a named model run identifier. Null for manual_override and batch_import methods.
reference: Internal ML Model Governance Standard; Internal Data Lineage Policy

description: Free-text field for additional context or commentary about this segment assignment. Used by CRM analysts and data scientists to document special circumstances, exceptions, or observations not captured by structured fields. Not used for automated processing.

The shopper's most recent Net Promoter Score (NPS) at the time of segment assignment, on a scale of 0 to 10. Captured as a point-in-time snapshot to contextualize the segmentation decision. Supports analysis of whether segment membership correlates with shopper satisfaction and advocacy.
reference: Internal NPS Measurement Standard; Internal Customer Experience Governance

description: Indicates whether the shopper has opted out of marketing communications associated with this segment. True means the shopper should not receive outbound marketing for this segment even if the assignment is active. Distinct from data_consent_flag which governs data processing consent.
reference: CAN-SPAM Act; CCPA – Opt-Out Rights; FTC – Advertising and Pricing Practices

description: Free-text explanation provided by the CRM analyst or marketing manager when assignment_method is 'manual_override'. Documents the business justification for bypassing the automated segmentation engine. Required for audit compliance when overriding ML or rule-based assignments.
reference: SOX Section 404 – Internal Controls; Internal Customer Segmentation Governance Policy

description: Indicates whether the shopper's segment assignment qualifies them for personalized digital experiences including eCommerce storefront personalization, app recommendations, and targeted email campaigns via Salesforce Commerce Cloud. Distinct from promotion_eligibility_flag which governs price-based offers.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud – Personalization Rules; Internal Digital Commerce Standard

description: Indicates whether the shopper is eligible to receive targeted promotions, TPR (Temporary Price Reduction) offers, or ad circular inclusions based on this segment assignment. True means the shopper qualifies for segment-specific promotional targeting. Supports the Promotions Management domain and FTC advertising compliance.
reference: FTC – Advertising and Pricing Practices; Internal Promotions Management Standard

description: Reference to the specific business rule set definition that triggered this assignment when assignment_method is 'rule_based'. Enables traceability of which rule configuration produced the membership. Null for ml_model, manual_override, and batch_import methods.

The high-level category of the segment to which the shopper is assigned. 'behavioral' segments are based on shopping behavior patterns; 'demographic' on age, income, or household composition; 'psychographic' on lifestyle and values; 'transactional' on purchase history and spend patterns; 'lifecycle' on shopper journey stage (new, active, lapsed, churned); 'program_based' on enrollment in specific programs such as SNAP, WIC, or pharmacy.
reference: Internal Customer Segmentation Taxonomy; Salesforce Commerce Cloud – Segment Classification

description: Numeric rank (1 = highest priority) indicating the relative priority of this segment assignment among all active segments for the shopper. Used to resolve conflicts when multiple segments offer competing promotions or personalization rules. Lower values indicate higher priority.
reference: Internal Customer Segmentation Governance Policy; NCR POS – Loyalty Integration Rules

description: Indicates whether this segment assignment is specifically associated with a SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) or WIC (Women, Infants, and Children) program participant segment. True flags the shopper as a government assistance program participant for targeted compliance reporting and eligible product promotions. Supports USDA and FTC regulatory requirements.
reference: USDA – SNAP and WIC Program Regulations; FTC – Advertising and Pricing Practices

description: The business event or condition that triggered the shopper's transition into this segment. 'purchase_event' indicates a qualifying transaction; 'loyalty_milestone' indicates reaching a loyalty tier threshold; 'model_refresh' indicates a scheduled ML model re-scoring run; 'manual_review' indicates a CRM analyst-initiated change; 'program_enrollment' indicates enrollment in SNAP, WIC, or pharmacy program; 'inactivity' indicates a lapse-based re-segmentation.
reference: Internal Customer Segmentation Governance Policy; NCR POS – Loyalty Integration

description: The date and time when this segment assignment record was last modified in the Databricks Silver Layer. Tracks updates such as status changes, expiry date extensions, or confidence score refreshes. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404 – Audit Trail Requirements; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Unique identifier for the household member association record. Primary key.
Reference to the household unit to which this member belongs.
Reference to the individual loyalty membership record associated with this household member.
Reference to the store location this household member most frequently shops at. Used for localized offers and inventory planning.
Total dollar amount spent by this household member in the current calendar year. Used for member value segmentation and household contribution analysis.
Total number of shopping visits or transactions by this household member in the current calendar year. Used for engagement and frequency analysis.
Date when this household member association was approved. Null if approval is pending or not required.

Approval status for this household member association. Pending members await primary member approval, rejected members were denied, revoked members had approval withdrawn, and approved members are active.

Level of household benefits this member is eligible to receive. Full tier receives all household benefits, partial receives select benefits, restricted has limited access, and none receives no shared benefits.
Physical or virtual loyalty card number assigned to this household member for POS (Point of Sale) identification. May be shared across household or unique per member depending on program rules.

Indicates whether this member inherits household communication preferences, has custom individual preferences, or suppresses all household communications. Enables personalized communication strategies within households.
Timestamp when this household member record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Indicates whether this household member has linked their individual digital wallet to the household loyalty account for seamless point accrual and redemption at POS (Point of Sale).
Indicates whether this household member has opted in to receive email communications about household loyalty benefits and offers. Only applies when communication_preference_override is custom.
Indicates whether this member can access and redeem household fuel rewards at fuel centers. Enables household-level fuel discount pooling and redemption tracking.

Current status of this household member association. Active members participate in household benefits, inactive members have left, suspended members are temporarily blocked, and pending members await approval.
Date when this member accepted the household invitation. Null if invitation is pending or was declined.
Unique code used to invite this member to join the household. Tracks invitation source and enables secure household joining workflows.
Date when the household invitation was sent to this member. Used to track invitation response time and follow-up campaigns.
Date when this member was added to the household. Used to track household growth and member tenure.
Timestamp when this household member record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data freshness monitoring.
Date of the most recent purchase or loyalty activity by this household member. Used to track member engagement and identify inactive members.
Date when this member left or was removed from the household. Null if the member is currently active in the household.
Total loyalty points this member has contributed to the household pool since joining. Used for household contribution analytics and member value assessment.
Total loyalty points this member has redeemed from the household pool since joining. Used to track member redemption behavior and benefit utilization.

Role of this member within the household loyalty structure. Primary members have full administrative rights, secondary members have shared benefits, dependents have limited access, and authorized users can transact on behalf of the household.
User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Free-text notes about this household member association. May include special circumstances, restrictions, or customer service history relevant to household management.

Override flag for household-level offer eligibility. Inherit uses household rules, eligible forces inclusion in household offers, ineligible excludes this member from household offers. Used for compliance or member preference scenarios.
Indicates whether this member can access household pharmacy loyalty benefits such as prescription discounts or health rewards. Subject to HIPAA privacy rules and state pharmacy regulations.
reference: HIPAA Privacy Rule 45 CFR 164.502, State Boards of Pharmacy

description: Indicates whether this member participates in household-level points pooling. When true, points earned by this member contribute to the shared household balance.
Percentage of points earned by this member that are contributed to the household pool. Range 0.00 to 100.00. Enables partial pooling scenarios where members retain some individual points.

Type of relationship this member has to the primary household member. Used for household composition analytics and targeted family offers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: spouse|partner|child|parent|sibling|roommate|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Indicates whether this household member has opted in to receive SMS text messages about household loyalty benefits and offers. Only applies when communication_preference_override is custom.
reference: TCPA (Telephone Consumer Protection Act), GDPR Article 7

description: Level of authority this member has to redeem household points or use household benefits. Full authority allows unrestricted redemption, limited requires approval thresholds, view-only can see but not transact, and none has no access.
Maximum dollar value or points this member can redeem per transaction or per period without additional approval. Null indicates no limit. Used for dependent and secondary member governance.
Primary key for membership
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Loyalty program cost allocation uses a cost center to budget and report program expenses per member.
Reference to the household group this membership belongs to, if the member is part of a household loyalty account where multiple members share points and benefits. Null for individual memberships.
The store location the member shops at most frequently, determined by transaction history. Used for localized offers and store-specific promotions.
The store location where the customer enrolled, if enrollment occurred in-store. Null for digital enrollments. Used for store-level enrollment performance tracking.
Foreign key linking to loyalty.program_config. Business justification: Membership participates in a specific loyalty program configuration; link to program_config for governance.
The membership ID of the existing member who referred this customer, if enrollment came through the member referral program. Null for non-referred enrollments. Used for referral reward attribution.
Reference to the customer who owns this loyalty membership. Links to the customer SSOT in the customer domain.
Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Membership belongs to a tier; replace string tier with foreign key for referential integrity.
Total purchase amount by the member in the current calendar or program year. Used for tier qualification thresholds and high-value member identification. Resets annually.
Total number of shopping trips or transactions by the member in the current calendar or program year. Used for frequency-based tier qualification and engagement scoring. Resets annually.

Segmentation of the member based on their predicted or actual customer lifetime value. High value members receive premium treatment and exclusive offers. At-risk members are targeted for retention campaigns.
The timestamp when this membership record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Indicates whether the membership card has been added to a digital wallet (Apple Wallet, Google Pay) for contactless loyalty identification at POS.
Indicates whether the member has opted in to receive physical mail communications including ad circulars and exclusive member offers.
Indicates whether the member has opted in to receive promotional emails and loyalty program communications via email.

The channel through which the customer enrolled in the loyalty program. Used for channel attribution analysis and enrollment campaign effectiveness measurement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: in_store|online|mobile_app|pharmacy|fuel_center|call_center|partner — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The date the customer enrolled in the loyalty program. Used to calculate membership tenure and anniversary-based rewards.
The current level or rank the member has achieved in the loyalty program gamification system through completing challenges, missions, or milestones. Higher levels unlock exclusive badges and rewards.
The most recent date the member earned or redeemed points through any transaction. Used for churn prediction and reactivation targeting.
The timestamp when this membership record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data freshness validation.
The most recent date the member made a purchase that earned loyalty points. Used for recency-based segmentation and lapsed member identification.
Cumulative total of all loyalty points earned since enrollment, including redeemed and expired points. Used for tier qualification and lifetime value analysis.
Cumulative total of all loyalty points redeemed since enrollment. Used for redemption rate analysis and program liability calculation.
The externally-known loyalty card number or membership identifier presented at POS and used for points accrual and redemption. This is the business identifier for the membership.

Current lifecycle status of the membership. Active memberships can earn and redeem points. Inactive memberships have no recent activity. Suspended memberships are temporarily blocked due to policy violations. Closed memberships are permanently terminated. Pending activation memberships are enrolled but not yet verified.
The date of the next scheduled points expiration event. Null if no points are scheduled to expire. Used for proactive member communication.

The most recent Net Promoter Score provided by the member through loyalty surveys. Scale of 0-10 where 9-10 are promoters, 7-8 are passives, 0-6 are detractors. Used for satisfaction tracking and churn prediction.
The date the member last completed an NPS survey. Used to determine survey recency and eligibility for follow-up surveys.
Current available loyalty points balance that can be redeemed for rewards, discounts, or merchandise. Updated in real-time with each transaction and redemption.
Number of points scheduled to expire within the next 30 days. Used for expiration reminder campaigns and breakage forecasting.
Indicates whether this member is the primary account holder for a household loyalty membership. Primary members have administrative privileges to add or remove household members.
Indicates whether the member has opted in to receive push notifications through the mobile app for personalized offers and program updates.

The source or campaign that led to the member enrollment. Examples: member referral program, social media campaign, in-store promotion, partner co-marketing. Used for acquisition cost analysis and campaign ROI measurement.
Indicates whether the member has opted in to receive promotional text messages and loyalty alerts via SMS.
reference: TCPA (Telephone Consumer Protection Act), GDPR Article 7

description: The date the current status became effective. Used for status history tracking and reactivation eligibility determination.
Detailed reason or code explaining the current membership status. Examples: voluntary closure, fraud suspension, inactivity timeout, duplicate account merge, deceased member.
The date the customer achieved their current tier level. Used for tier anniversary calculations and tier retention analysis.
The date the current tier expires if tier qualification thresholds are not maintained. Null for lifetime tiers. Used for tier retention campaigns and downgrade warnings.
Unique identifier for the loyalty program configuration record. Primary key.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Program configuration expenses are budgeted to a cost center to support financial planning and cost control.

Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Multi-banner grocery operators (e.g., Kroger, Albertsons) operate loyalty programs under distinct legal entities for regulatory compliance, tax reporting, and financial consolidation. Program config m
Default points earned per dollar spent across all categories before any multipliers or bonuses are applied.
Number of points automatically awarded to members during their birthday month as a retention incentive.
Rule defining how multiple earn bonuses (category, channel, tier) are combined when a transaction qualifies for more than one.
Date and time when this program configuration record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this program configuration (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).
Additional points bonus rate applied to last mile delivery orders.
Date when this program configuration version is superseded or retired. Null for currently active configurations.
Date when this program configuration version becomes active and begins governing member behavior.
Month and day (MM-DD format) when points expire annually if using fixed calendar expiry method. Null if not applicable.
Number of days after expiry date during which points can still be redeemed before permanent forfeiture.
Number of days of member inactivity after which points expire when using rolling inactivity expiry method. Null if not applicable.
Number of days before points expiry when the system sends notification alerts to members.

business_glossary_term: Fuel Redemption Cents Per Gallon Cap
description: Maximum discount in cents per gallon that can be applied to a single fuel transaction when redeeming points at fuel centers.
Maximum number of gallons eligible for discount in a single fuel transaction when redeeming loyalty points.
Indicates whether gamification features (badges, levels, challenges, leaderboards) are active for this program configuration.
Date and time when this program configuration record was most recently updated.
Date when this loyalty program configuration was first made available to customers.
Maximum number of points that can be redeemed in a single transaction to prevent excessive value extraction.
User identifier or email of the person who last modified this program configuration record for audit trail purposes.
Indicates whether this configuration supports members participating in multiple loyalty programs simultaneously (e.g., grocery + pharmacy + fuel).
Additional points bonus rate applied to purchases made through digital commerce channels (web, mobile app).
Indicates whether AI-driven personalized reward offers and recommendations are enabled for members in this program.
Additional points bonus rate applied to click-and-collect or curbside pickup orders.
Strategy used to determine when loyalty points expire (rolling window from last activity, fixed annual date, tier-dependent rules, or never).
Points multiplier applied to purchases of store-owned brand products to incentivize private label sales.
Detailed business description of the loyalty program purpose, target audience, and key benefits for internal reference.
Current lifecycle status of the loyalty program configuration.
Classification of the loyalty program by its primary business purpose and reward structure.
Minimum number of points a member must accumulate before they are eligible to redeem for rewards.
Conversion rate defining how many points equal one dollar of redemption value (e.g., 100 points = $1.00).
Number of points awarded to the new member who joins the program through a referral link or code.
Number of points awarded to the existing member who successfully refers a new member to the program.
Expected return on investment percentage target for this loyalty program configuration used for financial planning and performance evaluation.
Web address where the complete legal terms and conditions for this loyalty program version are published.
Cadence at which member tier status is recalculated based on points earned or spend thresholds.
Metric used to determine member tier qualification (current points balance, points earned in period, spend in period, or visit frequency).
Semantic version identifier for this program configuration (e.g., 1.0.0, 2.1.3) to support A/B testing and phased rollouts.
Number of points automatically awarded to new members upon successful enrollment in the loyalty program.
Unique identifier for the loyalty program tier. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.program_config. Business justification: tier is the reference master defining the loyalty program tier structure (Member, Silver, Gold, Platinum). program_config stores the complete configuration and business rules for each loyalty program.
Annual membership fee in USD required to maintain this tier. Zero for free tiers; positive amount for premium tiers. Used in billing and revenue recognition.
Comprehensive text description of all benefits and privileges associated with this tier. Used in customer communications, marketing materials, and mobile app displays.
Number of bonus loyalty points awarded to members of this tier on their birthday. Used for gamification and customer engagement.
Timestamp when this tier record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this tier (e.g., USD, CAD). Supports multi-currency loyalty programs.
Numeric order in which this tier should be displayed in user interfaces and reports. Used for consistent presentation across channels.
Business rule description explaining conditions under which customers are downgraded from this tier to a lower tier. Includes minimum points requirements and grace periods.
Date when this tier configuration is no longer active for new assignments. Null for currently active tiers. Supports tier retirement and program evolution.
Date when this tier configuration becomes active and available for customer assignment. Supports time-based tier launches and program changes.
Number of days over which points are accumulated and evaluated for tier qualification (e.g., 365 for annual evaluation, 90 for quarterly). Defines the rolling window for tier assignment calculations.
Indicates whether members of this tier receive exclusive promotional offers and early access to sales. True if exclusive offers are a tier benefit; false otherwise.
Indicates whether members of this tier are eligible for free delivery on online orders. True if free delivery is a tier benefit; false otherwise.
Fuel discount in cents per gallon offered to members of this tier at participating fuel centers. Integrates with fuel operations pricing systems.
Number of days customers retain this tier status after falling below the minimum points threshold before being downgraded. Provides buffer period for customers to re-qualify.
URL path to the tier icon image used in digital channels (mobile app, website, email). Supports visual tier recognition in customer-facing applications.
Timestamp when this tier record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.
Hexadecimal color code used to represent this tier in marketing materials, mobile apps, and customer communications (e.g., #FFD700 for Gold). Ensures consistent brand presentation.
Maximum number of loyalty points for this tier. Customers earning more than this threshold during the evaluation period qualify for the next higher tier. Null for the highest tier.
Minimum number of loyalty points required to qualify for this tier. Customers must earn at least this many points during the evaluation period to achieve or maintain this tier.
Username or identifier of the user who last modified this tier record. Supports audit trail and accountability for tier configuration changes.
Multiplier applied to base points earned by members of this tier (e.g., 1.0 for base tier, 1.5 for Silver, 2.0 for Gold). Used to calculate accelerated points accrual for higher tiers.
Indicates whether members of this tier receive priority customer service (e.g., dedicated phone line, faster response times). True if priority service is a tier benefit; false otherwise.
Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the tier (e.g., MEMBER, SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM). Used as business identifier in external systems and customer communications.
Human-readable display name of the loyalty tier (e.g., Member, Silver, Gold, Platinum). Used in customer-facing applications and marketing materials.

Numeric rank order of the tier within the loyalty program hierarchy. Lower numbers indicate higher status (e.g., 1=Platinum, 2=Gold, 3=Silver, 4=Member). Used for tier comparison and upgrade/downgrade logic.

Current lifecycle status of the tier. Active tiers are available for customer assignment; inactive tiers are no longer offered but may have existing members; deprecated tiers are being phased out; pending tiers are scheduled for future launch.
Business rule description explaining how customers qualify for upgrade to this tier from a lower tier. Includes points thresholds, evaluation periods, and any additional criteria.
Version number of this tier configuration. Incremented with each modification to support change history and rollback capabilities.
Number of bonus loyalty points awarded to customers when they first achieve this tier. Used to incentivize tier upgrades.
Unique identifier for each tier change event in the loyalty program. Primary key for the tier history transactional record.

Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Tier evaluation events (upgrades/downgrades) are assessed against fiscal periods for loyalty program P&L reporting and period-close reconciliation. Finance teams require tier-change costs bucketed by
Reference to the loyalty program member who experienced this tier change event.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: tier_history currently stores new_tier_code and new_tier_name as denormalized STRING columns. Adding new_tier_id as a FK to loyalty.tier.tier_id normalizes this relationship, allowing a JOIN to retrie
Reference to the omnichannel order that triggered this tier change event, if applicable. Null for in-store or system-initiated tier changes.
Reference to the specific Point of Sale (POS) transaction that triggered this tier change event, if applicable. Null for system-initiated or manual tier changes.

Foreign key linking to payment.payment_transaction. Business justification: Tier changes are often triggered by cumulative spend on payment transactions; linking supports tier‑evaluation reports and automated tier upgrades.
The date when the new tier status became active and the member began receiving benefits associated with the new tier level.
Precise timestamp when the tier change was applied in the loyalty system, used for real-time benefit activation and audit purposes.
The end date of the qualification period used to evaluate tier eligibility for this change event.
The start date of the qualification period used to evaluate tier eligibility for this change event (e.g., program year start, rolling 12-month window start).
The date when the new tier status is scheduled to expire, typically aligned with program year-end or qualification period end. Null for indefinite tier assignments.
Boolean flag indicating whether this tier change was manually adjusted by customer service or loyalty program administrators rather than automatically triggered by system rules.
Detailed explanation for manual tier adjustments, including customer service case reference, goodwill gesture context, or system error correction details. Null for automated tier changes.
The communication channel used to notify the member of the tier change: email, SMS (text message), app_push (mobile app notification), in_store (receipt or kiosk), or mail (postal).
Boolean flag indicating whether a tier change notification (email, SMS, app push) was successfully sent to the member.
Timestamp when the tier change notification was sent to the member through Customer Relationship Management (CRM) or loyalty communication channels.
The total loyalty points balance the member held at the exact moment of the tier change event, used for tier progression analytics and member lifecycle tracking.
The loyalty tier code the member held immediately before this change event (e.g., BRONZE, SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM).
Human-readable name of the previous loyalty tier for reporting and customer communication.
The loyalty program year during which this tier change occurred, used for annual tier reset and year-over-year progression analytics.
The cumulative spend amount that qualified the member for this tier change, measured over the qualification period. Null for non-spend-based tier changes.
The number of qualifying transactions completed during the evaluation period that contributed to this tier change. Null for non-transaction-based tier changes.
Timestamp when this tier history record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer, used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.
Timestamp when this tier history record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer, used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.
The operational system that originated this tier change record (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud, NCR POS Systems, custom loyalty platform).
The unique identifier of this tier change record in the source operational system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.
Boolean flag indicating whether all tier-specific benefits (discounts, free delivery, exclusive offers) have been successfully activated in the members profile.
Business-facing unique identifier for this tier change event, used for customer service inquiries and audit trails.

Standardized code indicating why the tier change occurred (e.g., EARNED_UPGRADE for qualifying spend threshold, ANNUAL_RESET for program year rollover, MANUAL_ADJ for customer service adjustment, DEMOTION for inactivity, PROMO_BOOST for promotional tier grant).
Detailed explanation of the business reason for the tier change, including any special circumstances or promotional context.
Current lifecycle status of this tier change record: active (currently in effect), expired (tier period ended), reversed (change was rolled back), or pending (scheduled but not yet effective).

Classification of the tier movement direction: upgrade (tier improvement), downgrade (tier reduction), lateral (same tier level but different program), initial_enrollment (first tier assignment), or reset (annual program reset).
Bonus loyalty points awarded to the member as a welcome gift upon achieving the new tier level. Zero or null if no bonus was granted.
Unique identifier for the loyalty reward redemption transaction. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Redemption events must be mapped to fiscal periods for loyalty liability reduction reporting and period-close reconciliation. Finance teams run period-end reports bucketing redemptions by fiscal perio

Foreign key linking to payment.gift_card. Business justification: Grocery loyalty programs allow points redemption for gift card issuance ('points-to-gift-card' redemption type). When redemption_type is GIFT_CARD, the loyalty_redemption must reference the specific g

Foreign key linking to loyalty.member_offer. Business justification: loyalty_redemption already references reward_offer_id (the offer template), but not the specific member_offer instance (the personalized assignment of that offer to the member). Adding member_offer_id
Reference to the loyalty membership that performed this redemption.
Reference to the omnichannel order (delivery or pickup) where the redemption was applied. Null for in-store POS transactions.

Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: Omnichannel redemption reconciliation: loyalty analysts and fulfillment ops must join redemptions to fulfillment_order to validate that delivery/pickup channel redemptions correspond to fulfilled orde

Foreign key linking to loyalty.points_transaction. Business justification: loyalty_redemption captures each redemption event. When a member redeems points, a corresponding points_transaction record is created to debit the points balance. Adding points_transaction_id to loyal
Reference to the pharmacy prescription where copay reduction was applied. Null for non-pharmacy redemptions. Contains protected health information.
Reference to the marketing campaign that generated the redeemed offer. Null for standard points redemptions.

Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_offer. Business justification: loyalty_redemption already has promo_campaign_id but lacks offer-level granularity. Grocery loyalty redemption reporting and offer performance dashboards require knowing which specific promo_offer was
Reference to the specific personalized reward offer or promotion that was redeemed. Null if redemption was a standard points-for-discount conversion.
Reference to the store location where the redemption occurred. Null for online-only redemptions.
External transaction identifier from the Point of Sale (POS) or Order Management System (OMS) where the redemption occurred. Links to NCR POS or Salesforce Commerce Cloud transaction.
reference: NCR POS Systems; Salesforce Commerce Cloud

description: Total transaction amount after the loyalty redemption discount was applied.
reference: NCR POS Systems; Salesforce Commerce Cloud

description: Total transaction amount before the loyalty redemption discount was applied. Provides context for redemption impact.
reference: NCR POS Systems; Salesforce Commerce Cloud

description: Channel or interface where the redemption occurred: in-store POS, online web, mobile app, fuel center, pharmacy, or curbside pickup.
Timestamp when this redemption record was first created in the loyalty system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the redemption value amount.
Type of device used to process the redemption: POS terminal, web browser, mobile app, in-store kiosk, or fuel pump interface.
Expiration date of the redeemed offer or reward. Null for non-expiring points redemptions. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Explanation of why the redemption failed or was reversed. Null for successful redemptions.
Cents-per-gallon discount applied to fuel purchase. Null for non-fuel redemptions.
Number of gallons of fuel purchased when fuel discount was applied. Null for non-fuel redemptions.

Current status of the redemption fulfillment: completed (successfully applied), pending (awaiting confirmation), failed (could not be applied), reversed (refunded/cancelled), or expired (offer expired before use).
Indicates whether this redemption was part of a gamification event or challenge (True) or a standard redemption (False).
Timestamp when this redemption record was last updated (e.g., status change, reversal). Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Indicates whether this redemption event qualifies the member for a Net Promoter Score (NPS) survey (True/False).
Member's loyalty points balance immediately after this redemption was processed.
Member's loyalty points balance immediately before this redemption was processed.
Number of loyalty points deducted from the member's balance for this redemption. Zero if redemption was a non-points reward (e.g., free item offer).

How the redemption was initiated: member-initiated (customer selected), auto-applied (system automatically applied best offer), cashier-applied (store associate applied), or system-triggered (rule-based application).
Date and time when the loyalty reward was redeemed at checkout. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Stock Keeping Unit identifier of the product associated with the redemption (for free item or product-specific discount redemptions). Null for non-product redemptions.

Monetary value of the redemption in local currency (dollar discount applied, fuel savings, copay reduction, or retail value of free item).

Reference to the loyalty membership record. Identifies which member this offer is assigned to.

Date when the offer was assigned to the member. Represents when the offer became available for this member to view or activate.

Account identifier from the source operational system. Used for cross-system reconciliation, data integration, and troubleshooting. May differ from the lakehouse primary key.
